evals.prompt: Generate a gateway controller for a GET /api/v1/shipment/:id/cost endpoint. It takes a numeric id param. No query, no body. It calls shipmentRPC.cost.getCost.query({ shipmentId: id }) and returns an object with fields: total (number), currency (string), breakdown (array of objects with label: string and amount: number). It needs auth and a permission check on 'ShipmentTracking' requiring the 'view' action. Add it to the existing shipment.route.ts.
evals.expected_output: Full controller file get-shipment-cost.controller.ts and route snippet to add to shipment.route.ts

assertions.description: Controller file imports typebox, schema-builder, status-code, and fastify types
Defines a params schema with id as Type.Number()
Reply schema uses ReplyBuilder.BuildSchemaWithData with total, currency, breakdown fields
Handler checks ShipmentTracking permission and throws ForbiddenError if 'view' missing
Calls shipmentRPC.cost.getCost.query with the correct arguments
Produces a fastify.get<...> route registration line for /api/v1/shipment/:id/cost

evals.prompt: I need to add a new endpoint to the gateway: DELETE /api/v1/shipment/:shipmentId/document/:documentId. It takes two numeric path params. No query or body. Calls shipmentRPC.document.deleteDocument.mutate({ shipmentId, documentId, userId }). Returns just a success message (no data payload). Auth required. Permission check on 'ShipmentTracking' with 'view' action. Put it in shipment.route.ts.
evals.expected_output: Full controller file delete-shipment-document.controller.ts and route snippet

assertions.description: Params schema includes both shipmentId and documentId as Type.Number()
No body schema is defined (DELETE with no body)
RPC call uses .mutate() not .query() since this is a DELETE

evals.prompt: Create a brand new gateway controller + route file for a POST /api/v1/shipment-calendar/:id/reminder endpoint. Params: id (number). Body: reminderDate (string), reminderType (enum of 'EMAIL' | 'PUSH' | 'SMS'). No query. Calls shipmentRPC.calendar.createReminder.mutate({ shipmentId: id, userId, ...body }). Returns object with id: number, createdAt: string. Auth required. No permission check needed. Create a new shipment-calendar-reminder.route.ts file and put the controller in controllers/shipment-calendar/.
evals.expected_output: Full controller file post-shipment-calendar-reminder.controller.ts and full new route file shipment-calendar-reminder.route.ts

assertions.description: ReminderType (or similar) enum is defined with EMAIL, PUSH, SMS values, used with Type.Enum
Body schema has reminderDate as Type.String() and reminderType as Type.Enum(...)
A complete route file is produced (not just a snippet), exporting a default async function
No permission check code in the handler (user explicitly said none needed)
Controller is placed in controllers/shipment-calendar/ folder
